_comment: debate-agent allowlist. Copy to ~/.config/debate-agent/allowlist.json (or point $DEBATE_AGENT_CONFIG at it) and edit for your machine. With no repo_roots set, EVERY request is rejected: the runner is closed by default and you open it deliberately here. The watch daemon re-reads this file for each new request, so edits apply to the next debate WITHOUT a restart; a malformed/half-saved edit is ignored (the last-good config stays in effect) and a warning is logged.
_providers_note: claude and codex by default. copilot is supported but OPT-IN and lower-assurance (the GitHub Copilot CLI has no OS filesystem sandbox); add "copilot" to this list only if you want it, and make sure `copilot login` is done.
_provider_aliases_note: Optional static aliases. Requests may put these ids in providers, preserving order, but the model/profile/base mapping comes only from this allowlist. Alias ids cannot shadow built-ins. profile is supported only for codex aliases and must be listed in profiles.codex.
_profiles_note: Only codex has local profiles the runner can honor (claude strips CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR; copilot has no profile concept here). Listing a profile for claude or copilot is an error; leave them empty to force the default account.
_capabilities_note: Child sandbox postures the runner may use. read_only_review = codex read-only sandbox + claude default permission (debate/critique, cannot edit) — this is the DEFAULT and the only one listed here. workspace_write = codex workspace-write + network + claude acceptEdits (lets a child edit the repo) is OPT-IN. remote_ops is delegate-only, Claude-only, and separately gated by remote_ops below; add it only if you want controlled Bash/SSH-style delegation.
_rate_limit_note: A failed child is classified rate_limited when its stderr/stdout matches one of these case-insensitive signatures. This is only a label; provider fallback can also happen for other failed launches such as nonzero_exit, timeout, missing_cli, or exception. These DEFAULTS are a starting point — verify them against your real claude/codex limit output and tune here; no code change needed. Listed providers override the built-in defaults; set a provider's list to [] to turn detection OFF for it. Omit this whole block to use the built-in defaults.
_fallback_note: Provider failure fallback policy. When a worker or planner cannot launch or fails to produce output, the daemon runs the SAME task on another available provider (same task, swap engine). enabled=false leaves failed launches to degrade/error without swapping. order is the preference for picking a substitute provider; it is filtered to allowlisted providers and defaults to `providers`. Every entry must be one of `providers`.
_delegate_note: High-level cli-delegator mailbox support is OFF by default. Enable only if you want sandboxed parents to submit ~/.cli-delegator/requests/*.json for this daemon to execute. First implementation supports mode=once only; supervised_loop/control remain future work. workspace_write delegate runs are capped separately because they open a larger blast radius.
_remote_ops_note: Delegate-only remote operations. Disabled by default. When enabled and capability=remote_ops is requested, the daemon only supports Claude providers and maps to --permission-mode default plus --allowedTools Read/Grep/Glob/Edit/Write/MultiEdit and Bash(<pattern>) for each static pattern below. SSH host restrictions are NOT enforced here; use SSH config, wrappers, network ACLs, or a dedicated runner for host-level control.
